items.title: Lansing sets hearing on 182-day data center moratorium after $120M proposal withdrawal
items.dek: The Lansing City Council will hold a public hearing July 13 on an ordinance imposing a 182-day pause on new data centers, citing high energy and water demands plus noise and emissions risks. The move follows the April withdrawal of a 24-megawatt, $120 million Deep Green project that had sought downtown rezoning and a heat-reuse deal with the Board of Water and Light.
items.source: Lansing City Council — Development & Planning agenda
items.findings: Ordinance adds Chapter 878 to impose 182-day moratorium barring building permits and zoning applications for data centers as primary or accessory use | per the draft: high-intensity use with significant energy/water consumption, noise, infrastructure demands, and likely environmental/public health impacts from backup generators | Deep Green 24 MW, $120M downtown proposal on re-zoned parking lots at Kalamazoo/Cedar/Larch would have paid city $1.4M plus nearly $1M annual property taxes and $1M+ to BWL | BWL heat-reuse plan scrapped with proposal; without it, utility faced $5M cost for new 4,000-sq-ft heating station in Wentworth Park | Two residents raised air quality, noise/vibration, energy, water, transparency, and independent review concerns at council meeting | Public hearing set for 7
items.perspective: The ordinance was referred to the Committee on Development and Planning at Monday's meeting after the Deep Green rezoning request was withdrawn April 6 before a conditional rezoning vote; city leaders indicated at the time the vote was not likely to get the six-vote supermajority required to pass. The July 13 hearing allows public comment before any council vote on the ordinance, which would take
items.judge_note: Document-based agenda preview with specific hearing date (July 13) and 182-day moratorium details for Lansing — concrete scheduled action.

items.title: Pere Marquette Township weighs year-long data center freeze
items.dek: Township leaders will vote on a one-year moratorium to examine how data centers would affect Ludington-area power lines and wells. A single large facility could pull as much electricity as thousands of homes. The delay lets residents weigh job promises against higher bills and land-use changes.
items.source_title: Pere Marquette Township to consider one-year data center moratorium - MasonCountyPress.com
items.judge_note: Concrete new development: Pere Marquette Township has a scheduled vote on a specific moratorium proposal, distinct from the already-covered Haring Township free
